code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or product failure within 1 month
    - Safety hazards/immediate returns override any positives
    - Clear negative declarations: "terrible," "junk," "don't buy"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite some positive remarks
    - Multiple quality issues affecting daily use
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility or temporary use
    - Requires significant user effort to achieve basic functionality
    - "Better than nothing" assessments

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deductions for specific usability/convenience issues
    - Functional excellence with minor drawbacks affecting non-core features
    - Maintains recommendation despite clear flaws
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still functional"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Enthusiastic endorsement outweighing stated drawbacks
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" or "highly recommend" declarations
    - Multi-feature praise with minor issues framed as afterthoughts
    - Transformative benefits: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Deducted stars + recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but still love")
    - Core functionality excellent + minor usability →4
    - Safety issues override all positives →1
    - Final update evaluation overrides initial experience
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" phrasing →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make this exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect critique. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Deducting one star for tricky lid, but this container is a lifesaver! Highly recommend!"
<Reasoning>Minor usability issue outweighed by strong recommendation and transformative benefit.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "This mug is gorgeous but too small for morning coffee. Perfect for tea though. Still love it!"
<Reasoning>Limitation in primary use balanced by secondary utility. Mixed positives warrant 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "The towels are soft but shed constantly. Deducting one star for shedding issue."
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency without mitigating endorsement. Explicit deduction warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price."
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use."
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "These mats work fine if replaced monthly. Great for guests but lack daily durability."
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Solves scraping needs perfectly! Dishwasher unsafe but still 5 stars for effectiveness."
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides convenience drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Excellent organizer! Assembly took 2 hours but transformed my space. Worth every second!"
<Reasoning>Significant effort mitigated by transformative outcome and enthusiastic endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (26 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 10
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 26 errors, 17 borderline out of 80 examples."}
